title: My Grief Is Allowed to Come and Go
subtitle: An affirmation for non-linear grief
excerpt: An affirmation for when grief arrives without warning, months or years after a loss. Use it to name the movement of grief as its natural pattern rather than a failure to recover.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education, drawing on the grief literature on non-linear grief (including Lois Tonkin's growing around grief model).

content.text: Grief does not finish and stay finished. It comes back in, often when least expected, triggered by a smell, a date, a song that was playing the day of the funeral. The return does not mean you went backward. It means you are carrying a loss that mattered.
My grief is allowed to come and go.

Say it when grief comes back in at a moment that catches you. Say it three times, hand on your heart. It does not make the grief smaller. It names the movement as something your grief is allowed to do.

Original to homemade.education. It draws on writing about non-linear grief, which sees grief not as something that shrinks but as something the griever grows around over time. See the sources block below.
